name: Charles Patel
technical_background: Basic scripting with Excel macros and simple VBA automation for document processing. Strong analytical reasoning from legal practice. Has used e-discovery tools with predictive coding (supervised ML) but did not build or configure the models. Understands compliance frameworks and risk assessment methodologies.

current_profile.summary: Junior litigation associate with 5 years of experience at a mid-size law firm specializing in technology and data privacy cases. Strong legal reasoning, writing, and compliance familiarity from working on GDPR, CCPA, and AI liability matters. Has used e-discovery tools with predictive coding (Relativity) and understands how supervised ML applies to document review. Comfortable with legal research databases (Westlaw, LexisNexis) and has used ChatGPT for drafting briefs and summarizing case law. Understands risk frameworks (NIST AI RMF at a conceptual level) from client advisory work. Basic Excel/VBA for document automation. No formal programming or data science training — minimal hands-on ML knowledge beyond e-discovery applications.
technical_skills: Legal brief writing and case summarization
current_profile.ai_experience: Has used e-discovery tools with predictive coding (Relativity) for document review — understands supervised ML concepts at a practical level but did not configure the models. Uses ChatGPT regularly for drafting briefs, summarizing case law, and brainstorming legal arguments. Has explored Westlaw AI-assisted research features. Familiar with AI liability and governance concepts from client advisory work on technology cases. Has not built, trained, or evaluated ML models.

learning_intent: Wants to work at the intersection of law and AI — helping organizations deploy AI safely and in compliance with emerging regulations. Goal: gain enough data science, ML evaluation, and security knowledge to audit AI models, interpret risk metrics, assess bias and fairness, and translate technical findings to legal stakeholders and regulators. Sees the convergence of AI governance and data science as a high-growth career path that leverages existing legal expertise.

target_jd.title: Data Scientist (AI Governance / Model Risk) - Legal Domain
requirements: Assess AI systems for legal, compliance, and operational risk using structured audit frameworks
Partner with engineers to implement controls for privacy, bias mitigation, and security
Develop evaluation plans and documentation for regulators and internal review boards
Communicate model limitations and risk tradeoffs to non-technical legal and business stakeholders
Design and execute bias and fairness testing for AI systems used in high-stakes decisions
Build and maintain model risk inventory and governance documentation
Monitor AI systems for drift, performance degradation, and compliance violations
Analyze AI outputs for hallucination risks in legal research and document generation
Develop data handling policies for AI training data (PII/PHI, retention, minimization)
Stay current with AI regulations (EU AI Act, state AI bills) and translate to actionable controls

expected_skill_gaps.description: Python basics, data handling (Pandas), SQL fundamentals for working with model audit datasets, and REST/API concepts to understand deployed AI systems.
Evaluation types (offline/online/red team), tracing and observability, prompt/model regression testing, and A/B testing for measuring AI system changes.
AI-specific governance: policy-to-controls mapping, AI regulations landscape, PII handling, data retention, and minimization practices.
AI security threat landscape, prompt injection awareness, data leakage prevention, and red teaming basics for AI system auditing.
Faithfulness evaluation and grounded citations for legal research assistants. Understanding RAG fundamentals for auditing retrieval-based AI systems.
Deepen understanding of model limitations, bias/fairness concepts, and IP/copyright implications for AI-generated legal content.
Legal-specific AI considerations: disclaimer and advice boundaries, hallucination evaluation frameworks for legal AI tools.
Writing AI project proposals for legal leadership and facilitating AI governance workshops with cross-functional teams.
